fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 72 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the function incorrectly returns True (allowed) instead of False (denied).

spec.expr: len(active__1) < limit or len(active__1) > limit or (len(active__1) == limit)

constraints.intent: When len(active) equals limit, the branch condition must be true so the function returns False,0; the bug is that `>` should be `>=`.
After the else branch (not denied), the number of active actions must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
Before using active in the comparison, if len(active)==limit the gate should deny — this constraint flags the boundary case that the strict inequality misses.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when the number of active actions is at or above the limit.
